behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: "request sent to the external API" targets the module→API-client handoff, not just a pure helper. Concrete miss at Unit: a payload-builder unit test can pass while an adapter/serializer drops or renames the taxonomy field before send. Boundary type: handoff-state. Rejection gate for higher levels: no behavior text requires real HTTP/OS/subprocess (so not System), and this check does not depend on model judgment/output quality (so not Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that records outbound requests; invoke classify() with a known taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full, exact label set (no omissions/reordering loss if order is significant by contract).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: "When the API returns... the module returns" is response handoff across API-client result parsing and normalization. Concrete miss at Unit: a pure normalizer test can pass while integration code bypasses normalization and forwards raw provider label. Boundary type: handoff-state. Rejection gate for higher levels: no real OS/network requirement is stated; no dependence on real model quality (only handling an explicit invalid value).
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub provider returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"); call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and raw value is not leaked.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: explicitly scoped to "normalization step" with "identical raw API responses"; this is deterministic local transformation logic. Concrete miss at lower level: none (Unit is floor). Rejection gate for higher levels: text disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow because no real infra or model decision is required once raw response is fixed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants if relevant) and assert output label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: "repeated live API calls" and consistency of returned label depend on real provider/model outputs. Concrete miss at System: a deterministic fake/live transport check can pass while real model classification drifts across calls. Boundary type: model-output. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for identical input over a short window; record raw provider labels and module outputs; assert module output changes only when raw provider label changes, and quantify stability rate threshold for alerting.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: "before dispatch" requires construction + dispatch gating interaction. Concrete miss at Unit: token-budget helper can pass while caller still dispatches oversized requests due to wiring bug. Boundary type: handoff-state. Rejection gate for higher levels: no need for real HTTP/OS; no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: With a stub API client and controlled long input, verify policy behavior (truncate or reject) is applied and dispatch occurs only with compliant payload (or not at all for reject policy).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: "Provider-reported token usage" comes from real external AI API responses. Concrete miss at Integration: stubbed usage values will always satisfy expected budget and cannot detect real provider/tokenizer drift or hidden overhead. Boundary type: model-output (real AI API output field). Rejection gate for Workflow: single call metric check, not multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls on representative documents and assert provider-reported usage field is <= configured budget; fail on over-budget observations and track distribution over multiple runs.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evidence: module behavior on provider signal (rate limit) is retry orchestration across client + backoff policy. Concrete miss at Unit: isolated retry function can pass while classifier path fails to invoke it or propagates error prematurely. Boundary type: handoff-state. Rejection gate for higher levels: real OS/network is not required because deterministic stub responses (429 then success) can reproduce failure; no dependence on model judgment/output semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Configure stub provider to return rate-limit response then success; use controllable clock/backoff scheduler; assert classify() waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing the initial error.
